skill.name: three
skill.description: Three.js and WebGL adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when creating deterministic Three.js scenes, WebGL canvas layers, AnimationMixer timelines, camera motion, shader-driven visuals, or canvas renders that respond to HyperFrames hf-seek events.
skill.summary: Build deterministic Three.js 3D scenes that render from HyperFrames seek events instead of wall-clock time.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 26 static analysis findings are false positives. The 20 backtick execution detections are markdown code fence delimiters, inline code formatting, and JavaScript template literals in documentation code examples, not Ruby shell execution. The 3 hardcoded URL findings point to a legitimate Three.js CDN import and official documentation links. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance blockers are misclassifications of YAML frontmatter and standard coding guidelines. This is a documentation skill for Three.js integration with HyperFrames with no security concerns.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 20 instances of backtick characters as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard markdown formatting: code fence delimiters, inline code spans, and JavaScript template literals within documentation code examples. No actual command execution is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate References
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 3 hardcoded URLs. One is a jsdelivr CDN import for the Three.js library (standard practice), and two are official Three.js documentation links. No data exfiltration or malicious endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: YAML Frontmatter Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. Line 3 contains YAML frontmatter description metadata, not any cryptographic code. This is a false positive from pattern matching.

low_findings.title: Coding Guidelines Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged coding best practice instructions as system reconnaissance. Lines 16 and 85 contain standard Three.js development guidelines about avoiding requestAnimationFrame and using time-based rendering.

content.user_title: Create Three.js 3D scenes for HyperFrames video compositions
content.value_statement: Three.js developers struggle to make 3D animations deterministic for video rendering. This skill provides the patterns and code needed to drive Three.js scenes from HyperFrames seek events so every frame renders consistently.
actual_capabilities: Creates deterministic Three.js scenes that render exact frames from HyperFrames seek events
Provides AnimationMixer seeking patterns for GLTF animation clip playback on the video timeline
Guides time-driven camera motion and scene animation instead of wall-clock-based updates
Shows WebGL renderer configuration for deterministic video output across devices
Demonstrates proper asset loading strategy for render-critical seeking
References validation commands to check Three.js composition correctness
limitations: Does not manage the Three.js scene lifecycle or asset loading
Cannot resolve issues from loading remote assets during seek-time rendering
Post-processing effects that depend on previous frame history may not produce deterministic output
Requires all assets to be loaded before render-critical seeking begins

use_cases.title: Product visualization for video ads
use_cases.description: A video editor creates product spin animations with Three.js for marketing videos. The skill helps set up deterministic rotation and camera paths that render consistently at each frame.

use_cases.title: Procedural 3D backgrounds for compositions
use_cases.description: A motion designer needs a 3D particle system or shader plate as a video background layer. The skill provides the pattern to synchronize procedural animations with video frames.

use_cases.title: GLTF character animation for explainer videos
use_cases.description: An animator imports GLTF models with animation clips and needs them to play in sync with the video timeline. The skill shows how to seek AnimationMixer from HyperFrames time values.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Three.js scene with seek-based rendering
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HyperFrames-compatible Three.js scene with a rotating mesh. Import Three.js from the jsdelivr CDN, set up the renderer with alpha and antialias, and use the hf-seek event to drive rotation based on time.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new Three.js canvas layer in a HyperFrames composition

prompt_templates.title: GLTF animation clip playback with mixer
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to play a GLTF animation clip in sync with the HyperFrames timeline. Show me the AnimationMixer pattern where mixer.setTime(time) is called from the hf-seek event handler for deterministic playback.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding character animation to a video composition

prompt_templates.title: Time-driven camera motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a Three.js camera that moves along a path based on HyperFrames time. Use trigonometric functions on the time value to create smooth orbiting or dolly motion that renders deterministically.
prompt_templates.scenario: Designing dynamic camera shots for a video

prompt_templates.title: Multiple synchronized animation mixers
prompt_templates.prompt: I have several GLTF models each with their own AnimationMixer. Show me how to seek all mixers from the same hf-seek time value so they stay synchronized throughout the video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinating multiple animated objects in one composition

output_examples.input: Create a rotating 3D cube that responds to HyperFrames seek events
output_examples.output: The skill produces an HTML document with a Three.js scene set up with WebGL renderer, a rotating mesh object, and event listeners that drive rotation from the hf-seek event detail time value instead of requestAnimationFrame.

output_examples.input: Play a GLTF character animation synced to the video timeline
output_examples.output: The skill generates a JavaScript pattern showing how to load GLTF models, create AnimationMixer instances, and call mixer.setTime(time) inside the hf-seek event handler for deterministic clip playback.

output_examples.input: Set up a camera orbit that matches video duration
output_examples.output: The skill provides a camera motion pattern where orbit position is derived from the HyperFrames time value using sine and cosine functions, ensuring every frame renders the same camera angle regardless of device performance.

best_practices: Load all models, textures, and HDRIs before render-critical seeking to avoid delays at frame render time
Use renderer.setSize with setPixelRatio set to 1 for deterministic output across all devices
Derive all scene updates from the time value in the hf-seek event, never from Date.now or performance.now
anti_patterns: Using requestAnimationFrame or setAnimationLoop as the source of truth for render-critical motion instead of hf-seek events
Loading remote models or textures during seek-time rendering which causes frame drops and non-deterministic output
Using Date.now or performance.now for scene state updates which produces different results on each render pass

faq.question: Does the Three.js adapter manage my scene?
faq.answer: No. The adapter only publishes HyperFrames time and dispatches seek events. You own the scene, camera, renderer, and all assets.

faq.question: Can I use requestAnimationFrame for animations?
faq.answer: Avoid requestAnimationFrame as the source of truth for render-critical motion. Use hf-seek events to render exact frames from HyperFrames time.

faq.question: How do I load GLTF models with animations?
faq.answer: Load models before render-critical seeking. In the hf-seek handler, call mixer.setTime(time) on each AnimationMixer to seek to the correct frame.

faq.question: What renderer settings should I use for video?
faq.answer: Use setPixelRatio set to 1 and setSize to your composition frame dimensions. Pin renderer size for consistent output across devices.

faq.question: Can I use post-processing effects?
faq.answer: Yes, but avoid effects that depend on previous frame history unless you can reconstruct state from time alone.

faq.question: How do I validate a Three.js composition?
faq.answer: Run npx hyperframes lint and npx hyperframes validate to check your composition for common issues.
